days.title: Arrival in Tokyo - West Tokyo
days.subtitle: Touching down and exploring Shinjuku to Shibuya

timeline.title: Arrive at Narita Airport
timeline.detail: Flight arrives at 08:25am at Narita Airport. After arriving, take the Narita Express (N'EX) or Airport Limousine Bus to Shinjuku Station. Journey takes approximately 60-90 minutes depending on traffic. Keep your luggage organized and be ready for check-in at the Airbnb around 15:00-16:00. Ensure all 6 travelers stay together during transfer.
timeline.mapQuery: Narita Airport to Shinjuku Station, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Luggage Storage and Light Lunch
timeline.detail: Store luggage at coin lockers near Shinjuku Station (South Exit area has plenty of large lockers for 20-30kg bags). Enjoy a light lunch at a nearby ramen or udon shop. This gives you time to refresh, grab cash from ATM, and plan the afternoon route before heading to Shinjuku Gyoen.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Station South Exit, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
timeline.detail: Explore this beautiful 58-hectare garden featuring Japanese, English, and French-style landscapes. Perfect for photography with seasonal flowers and peaceful walking paths. Entry costs around 500 yen per person. Spend 90 minutes walking, photographing, and enjoying the tranquil atmosphere away from Tokyo's bustling streets. Great warm-up activity after travel.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Meiji Jingu Shrine Visit
timeline.detail: Walk through the peaceful forested path to Japan's most visited shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji. No entrance fee. Take time to appreciate the torii gates, purchase omamori (charms), and explore the shrine grounds. This is approximately 15-20 minutes walk from Shinjuku Gyoen. Perfect for cultural photography and understanding Japanese spiritual traditions.
timeline.mapQuery: Meiji Jingu Shrine, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Check-in at Airbnb Ōkubo Area
timeline.detail: Head back to the Airbnb apartment at 1-chōme-9-17 Ōkubo, Shinjuku City. It's a 10-minute walk from Shinjuku Station or one train stop. Settle in, unpack, assign rooms, and familiarize yourselves with the property. Check the washing machine location for laundry needs. Recharge devices and coordinate the evening plan with your group.
timeline.mapQuery: 1-chōme-9-17 Ōkubo, Shinjuku City, Tokyo Prefecture 169-0072, Japan

timeline.title: Omotesando Shopping & Shibuya Crossing
timeline.detail: Take the train to Omotesando (Harajuku area) for upscale shopping and trendy boutiques. Then head to iconic Shibuya Crossing, the world's busiest pedestrian crossing. Best photographed from Starbucks or overlooking platform. Visit Hachiko statue nearby. This area offers excellent photo opportunities and showcases modern Tokyo culture.
timeline.mapQuery: Omotesando, Shibuya Crossing, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Pokémon Center Shibuya and Dinner
timeline.detail: Explore the Pokémon Center Shibuya for exclusive merchandise and fun photo opportunities. It's located inside the 109 Building. Afterward, grab dinner at a nearby izakaya or ramen shop in Shibuya area. Budget 1,500-3,000 yen per person for quality Japanese cuisine. Great spot for group photos and enjoying Japanese nightlife atmosphere.
timeline.mapQuery: Pokémon Center Shibuya, 109 Building, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Kabukicho and Golden Gai Nightlife
timeline.detail: Head to Kabukicho in Shinjuku, Tokyo's famous red light district with neon signs and entertainment venues. Then explore Golden Gai, a narrow alley with tiny traditional bars featuring unique atmospheres. Many bars fit 5-7 people comfortably. Drinks cost 800-1,500 yen. Experience authentic Tokyo nightlife culture and socialize with locals in intimate settings.
timeline.mapQuery: Kabukicho, Golden Gai, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Return to Airbnb and Rest
timeline.detail: Head back to the Ōkubo Airbnb area using the train. It's just one stop from Shinjuku Station. Settle in for the night, prepare for Day 2, and ensure everyone has charged their devices and cameras. Discuss plans for tomorrow's Old Tokyo and Akihabara exploration. Get adequate rest for an active week ahead.
timeline.mapQuery: 1-chōme-9-17 Ōkubo, Shinjuku City, Tokyo Prefecture 169-0072, Japan

localTips: Get a Suica/Pasmo card at Narita Express counters — ¥2,000 deposit, valid on all trains and buses.
Narita Express (N'EX) takes 60 minutes to Shinjuku Station; buy round-trip ticket for ¥3,070 return.
Shinjuku is the busiest station in the world — follow signage carefully to Ōkubo area exit.
Most restaurants accept cash only; find 7-Eleven ATMs for international cards.
Download Google Maps offline map of Tokyo before arrival for reliable navigation without data usage.

days.title: Old Tokyo & Akihabara
days.subtitle: Traditional temples and electric town exploration

timeline.title: Breakfast & Hotel Checkout Prep
timeline.detail: Wake up and have a light breakfast at the Airbnb in Ōkubo area. Prepare for the day by organizing belongings, confirming group departure time, and ensuring everyone has charged phones and cameras. Review the day's itinerary and discuss meeting points in case the group splits.

timeline.title: Train Journey to Asakusa
timeline.detail: Depart from Ōkubo area and take the train to Asakusa Station (approximately 20-30 minutes via Shinjuku Station). Navigate the Tokyo Metro system using IC cards or purchased tickets. The journey will pass through central Tokyo, giving you a feel for the city's scale and efficiency.
timeline.mapQuery: Ōkubo, Shinjuku to Asakusa Station, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Senso-ji Temple & Nakamise Street Shopping
timeline.detail: Explore the iconic Senso-ji Temple, Tokyo's oldest temple with its famous red lantern and pagoda. Browse the bustling Nakamise shopping street featuring traditional souvenirs, snacks, and handcrafted goods. Arrive early to avoid peak crowds. Take photos with the temple's iconic architecture and soak in the spiritual atmosphere while shopping for company gifts.
timeline.mapQuery: Senso-ji Temple, Asakusa, Taito, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Traditional Eel (Unagi) Lunch
timeline.detail: Enjoy authentic Japanese eel lunch at a traditional restaurant near Asakusa. This is a specialty dish grilled over charcoal with sweet sauce, served over rice. Book ahead or arrive early as popular spots get crowded. This meal is a quintessential Tokyo experience and provides excellent photo opportunities for the company trip album.
timeline.mapQuery: Unagi restaurant, Asakusa, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Train to Yanaka Ginza
timeline.detail: Take the train from Asakusa to Yanaka area (approximately 15-20 minutes). This charming neighborhood retains old Tokyo character with narrow streets and vintage shops. The journey through different train lines showcases Tokyo's excellent public transportation system. Yanaka Ginza is a nostalgic shopping street that offers a stark contrast to modern Tokyo.
timeline.mapQuery: Asakusa Station to Yanaka, Taito, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Yanaka Ginza Shopping & Exploration
timeline.detail: Stroll through Yanaka Ginza, a retro shopping street featuring vintage shops, local eateries, and craft stores. This area represents traditional Tokyo atmosphere before modernization. Browse boutiques, try local snacks, and capture photographs of the preserved architecture and street scenes. Great for understanding Tokyo's cultural heritage and finding unique souvenirs.
timeline.mapQuery: Yanaka Ginza, Taito, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Train to Akihabara
timeline.detail: Travel by train from Yanaka to Akihabara Station (approximately 10-15 minutes via Uguisudani or nearby stations). Akihabara is Tokyo's famous electric and anime district. Upon arrival, use the station map to orient yourself and decide on group strategy for exploring the many shops and arcades.
timeline.mapQuery: Yanaka to Akihabara Station, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Akihabara Anime & Electric District Exploration
timeline.detail: Explore Akihabara's vibrant anime district featuring multi-story electronics shops, anime merchandise stores, gaming arcades, and maid cafes. Visit major stores like Yodobashi, Don Quijote, and smaller niche shops. Browse anime figurines, video games, tech gadgets, and pop culture merchandise. The district is heavily photographed and perfect for company photos showcasing Tokyo's contemporary culture.
timeline.mapQuery: Akihabara, Chiyoda, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Dinner in Akihabara
timeline.detail: Enjoy dinner at one of Akihabara's themed restaurants or casual eateries offering ramen, tonkatsu, or conveyor belt sushi. Many restaurants offer interactive dining experiences and unique atmospheres. This meal allows the group to relax after a full day of walking and exploration while experiencing contemporary Japanese dining culture.
timeline.mapQuery: Restaurant, Akihabara, Chiyoda, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Return to Ōkubo Airbnb
timeline.detail: Take the train back from Akihabara to Shinjuku, then to Ōkubo area (approximately 25-35 minutes total journey time). The evening return journey offers insight into Tokyo's nightlife as the city lights up. Arrive at the Airbnb, review the day's photos, do laundry if needed using the property's washing machine, and prepare for Day 3's Kawagoe half-day trip.
timeline.mapQuery: Akihabara Station to Ōkubo, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

localTips: Senso-ji opens at 6am; arrive early to avoid crowds and catch monks performing morning rituals.
Nakamise Street vendors prefer cash; budget ¥500-1,500 per snack item (dango, manju, senbei).
Yanaka Ginza is a hidden local shopping street — no tourists, authentic 1970s Japanese vibe.
Akihabara has 8 major anime/manga shops; allow 2-3 hours to explore multiple floors.
Last train from Akihabara to Shinjuku is 00:34; plan nightlife accordingly.

days.title: Kawagoe Day Trip & Free Evening
days.subtitle: Historic merchant town and unscheduled Tokyo exploration

timeline.title: Wake up and breakfast at Airbnb
timeline.detail: Prepare a light breakfast at the Airbnb in Ōkubo area. Pack light snacks, water, and cameras for the day trip. Confirm all 6 travelers are ready for departure. Check weather (expect 10-20°C) and wear appropriate layers for spring in Kawagoe.
timeline.mapQuery: 1-chōme-9-17 Ōkubo, Shinjuku City, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Train from Shinjuku to Kawagoe Station
timeline.detail: Travel from Shinjuku Station to Kawagoe Station via JR Shonan-Shinjuku Line or local trains (approximately 45-50 minutes). Purchase IC cards or day passes for all 6 travelers. Kawagoe is a historic merchant town about 30km northwest of Tokyo, famous for its well-preserved Edo-period architecture and traditional culture.
timeline.mapQuery: Kawagoe Station, Saitama, Japan

timeline.title: Explore Kurazukuri Street
timeline.detail: Walk along the historic Kurazukuri Street featuring traditional merchant houses (kurazukuri-style buildings) with distinctive black walls and white plaster. These well-preserved wooden structures date back 100+ years. Browse small shops selling local crafts, sweets, and souvenirs. Take photographs of the charming streetscape and historic architecture for approximately 45 minutes.
timeline.mapQuery: Kurazukuri Street, Kawagoe, Saitama, Japan

timeline.title: Visit Toki no Kane Bell Tower
timeline.detail: Climb to the iconic Toki no Kane (Fire Lookout Tower) bell tower, a symbol of Kawagoe standing 16 meters tall. The current structure dates to 1893. The bell rings four times daily. Entry is free. Enjoy panoramic views of Kawagoe's historic district and narrow streets from the top. Spend 20-30 minutes here exploring and photographing the surrounding area.
timeline.mapQuery: Toki no Kane Bell Tower, Kawagoe, Saitama, Japan

timeline.title: Candy Alley (Kashiya Yokocho) exploration and snacks
timeline.detail: Walk through Kashiya Yokocho (Candy Alley), a charming narrow alley with over 15 traditional candy and snack shops selling handmade Japanese sweets, chocolates, and regional specialties. Sample local treats and purchase souvenirs for your group of 6. This atmospheric alley captures old-Japan ambiance with wooden facades and vintage signage. Allow 45 minutes for browsing and tasting.
timeline.mapQuery: Kashiya Yokocho, Kawagoe, Saitama, Japan

timeline.title: Lunch at local Kawagoe restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional Japanese lunch at a local restaurant near Kurazukuri Street area. Try Kawagoe specialties such as unagi (eel), soba noodles, or sweet potato dishes for which the region is famous. Budget approximately 1,000-2,000 JPY per person. Ensure the restaurant can accommodate your group of 6 and cater to any dietary preferences.
timeline.mapQuery: Kurazukuri Street restaurants, Kawagoe, Saitama, Japan

timeline.title: Hikawa Shrine visit and exploration
timeline.detail: Visit Hikawa Shrine (Hikawa Jinja), one of Japan's oldest shrines established in 2400 AD according to legend, located in central Kawagoe. Walk through the peaceful shrine grounds, observe traditional Japanese architecture, and participate in customs like buying omamori (protective amulets). The shrine is known for matchmaking blessings. Spend approximately 40 minutes exploring the serene atmosphere and taking photographs.
timeline.mapQuery: Hikawa Shrine, Kawagoe, Saitama, Japan

timeline.title: Shingashi River cherry blossom viewing and riverside walk
timeline.detail: Stroll along the beautiful Shingashi River lined with cherry blossom trees (sakura). In early April, blossoms are likely at peak bloom or falling as petals. Walk along the scenic riverside path perfect for photography. The gentle water reflections and overhanging cherry branches create picturesque scenes ideal for group photos. Budget 60 minutes for leisurely walking and enjoying the natural beauty.
timeline.mapQuery: Shingashi River, Kawagoe, Saitama, Japan

timeline.title: Return train to Tokyo Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Board return train from Kawagoe Station back to Shinjuku Station (approximately 50 minutes). Relax after a full day of sightseeing. Arrive back in Shinjuku by approximately 5:00 PM, allowing the entire group free evening to explore Tokyo independently or rest at the Airbnb before departure the next day.
timeline.mapQuery: Kawagoe Station to Shinjuku Station, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Free evening in Tokyo - independent exploration
timeline.detail: After returning to base camp in Ōkubo area, Shinjuku, the entire group has a free evening to explore Tokyo independently. Options include visiting nearby neighborhoods (Shinjuku, Shibuya, Harajuku), dining at restaurants of choice, enjoying nightlife, shopping, or relaxing at the Airbnb. Recommend exploring neighborhood ramen shops, convenience stores, or local izakayas within walking distance of base.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

localTips: Take JR Kawagoe Line from Shinjuku to Hon-Kawagoe Station (39 minutes, ¥750 one-way).
Rent a bicycle at Hon-Kawagoe Station for ¥500/day — all main attractions are within 2km.
Kurazukuri merchant houses are open 09:30-17:00; admission ¥500 each (optional paid tours).
Candy Alley (Kashiya Yokocho) has 20+ tiny shops — buy omiyage (souvenirs) here for great prices.
Return bus/train departs 16:00-17:00 for evening flexibility — book flexible day tickets, not round-trip.

days.title: Mt Fuji Day Trip - Kawaguchiko
days.subtitle: Full day at Japan's iconic peak with lakeside views

timeline.title: Wake up and breakfast at Airbnb
timeline.detail: Early rise to prepare for the Mt Fuji day trip. Have breakfast at the Ōkubo Airbnb with the group. Pack light snacks, water, and camera equipment. Check weather conditions for Kawaguchiko and wear appropriate layering (10-20°C range). Ensure all 6 travelers are ready to depart by 8:00 AM sharp.
timeline.mapQuery: 1-chōme-9-17 Ōkubo, Shinjuku City, Tokyo Prefecture 169-0072, Japan

timeline.title: Travel to Busta Shinjuku bus station
timeline.detail: Walk or take local train from Ōkubo Airbnb to Busta Shinjuku (approx 15-20 minutes walk or 5 minutes by train). Confirm all 6 group members are present and have their tickets. Arrive at least 20 minutes before departure to secure seats and organize luggage.
timeline.mapQuery: Busta Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Depart Busta Shinjuku to Kawaguchiko
timeline.detail: Board the direct highway bus from Busta Shinjuku heading to Kawaguchiko (approximately 90-120 minutes journey). Seats are comfortable with restroom facilities. Enjoy scenic views of Tokyo fading into countryside as you approach Mt Fuji. Bring entertainment or rest during the journey.
timeline.mapQuery: Busta Shinjuku to Kawaguchiko, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Arrive at Kawaguchiko Station and explore lakeside
timeline.detail: Arrive at Kawaguchiko Station. Start with a scenic lakeside walk along Lake Kawaguchi to acclimatize and capture photographs of Mt Fuji reflected in the water. The views are particularly stunning on clear days. Stop at viewpoints and enjoy the fresh mountain air. Walk duration approximately 45 minutes to 1 hour.
timeline.mapQuery: Kawaguchiko Station, Yamanashi, Japan

timeline.title: Mt Fuji Ropeway experience
timeline.detail: Take the Mt Fuji Ropeway (Kawaguchiko Ropeway) for spectacular elevated views of the iconic mountain and surrounding lakes. The 7-minute ride reaches an elevation of approximately 1,075 meters. At the summit station, enjoy 360-degree panoramic views and explore the scenic observation deck. Perfect photography opportunity. Total experience: 45 minutes to 1 hour.
timeline.mapQuery: Mt Fuji Ropeway, Kawaguchiko, Yamanashi, Japan

timeline.title: Lunch at Kawaguchiko lakeside restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy traditional Japanese lunch at a lakeside restaurant with Mt Fuji views. Try local specialties such as houtou noodles, fresh fish from the lake, or tempura. Many restaurants offer set meals with excellent value. Seating for groups of 6 can be arranged if you book ahead or arrive early. Allow 1 hour for comfortable dining experience.
timeline.mapQuery: Kawaguchiko lakeside restaurants, Yamanashi, Japan

timeline.title: Oishi Park visit and nature walk
timeline.detail: Visit Oishi Park for one of the most famous Mt Fuji viewing spots in Japan. The park features beautiful seasonal flowers (cherry blossoms in spring, lavender in summer, cosmos in autumn). Walk through the landscaped gardens with Mt Fuji framing the background. Excellent photography location. Spend 1-1.5 hours exploring and capturing images.
timeline.mapQuery: Oishi Park, Kawaguchiko, Yamanashi, Japan

timeline.title: Shopping and refreshments at Kawaguchiko market
timeline.detail: Browse local shops near Kawaguchiko Station selling Mt Fuji souvenirs, regional crafts, and snacks. Visit convenience stores or local cafes for refreshments and light snacks before the return journey. Excellent opportunity to purchase gifts for colleagues and mementos of the trip. Allow 45 minutes for browsing and shopping.
timeline.mapQuery: Kawaguchiko Town Center, Yamanashi, Japan

timeline.title: Depart Kawaguchiko Station for Tokyo
timeline.detail: Board the return highway bus from Kawaguchiko Station heading back to Busta Shinjuku (approximately 90-120 minutes journey). Recount all 6 group members before departure. Relax and reflect on the Mt Fuji experience during the ride. Bus arrives back at Busta Shinjuku around 18:30-19:00.
timeline.mapQuery: Kawaguchiko Station to Busta Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Return to Airbnb and dinner planning
timeline.detail: Arrive back at Ōkubo Airbnb around 19:15 after the full day Mt Fuji adventure. Freshen up and rest briefly. The group can decide to eat dinner as a team at a nearby Shinjuku restaurant or order takeaway back at the Airbnb. Share photos and experiences from the day. Ensure everyone is properly hydrated and rested after the long day trip.
timeline.mapQuery: 1-chōme-9-17 Ōkubo, Shinjuku City, Tokyo Prefecture 169-0072, Japan

localTips: Book Mt Fuji day trip coach from Busta Shinjuku online 3-5 days ahead via Fujikyu Sightseeing website.
Round-trip bus from Shinjuku takes 2.5 hours each way; depart 08:30, return 18:00 (¥2,600/person).
Mt Fuji Ropeway costs ¥900 return; queues peak 11am-2pm so go early or late afternoon.
Oishi Park is 400m walk from Kawaguchiko Station; sakura blooms may still be visible in early April.
Pack layers — mountain area is 5-10°C cooler than Tokyo; bring light jacket even in spring.

days.title: Modern Tokyo - Tsukiji & Skyline
days.subtitle: City highlights, shopping, and interactive fun

timeline.title: Breakfast & Prepare for Day
timeline.detail: Wake up at the Ōkubo Area Airbnb in Shinjuku. Have a light breakfast using convenience store items or prepare simple meals at the apartment. Check weather (expect 10-20°C) and dress in layers. Confirm group meeting point and transport method to Tsukiji. Ensure all phones are charged for photography throughout the day.

timeline.title: Travel to Tsukiji Outer Market
timeline.detail: Take the Metro from Shinjuku Station to Tsukiji-Shijo Station (Hibiya Line, approximately 15 minutes). Exit and walk 5 minutes to Tsukiji Outer Market. Travel as a group of 6 to stay together. Purchase IC cards if not already done for seamless metro transfers throughout the day.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsukiji Outer Market, Chuo Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Tsukiji Outer Market Exploration & Food
timeline.detail: Explore the vibrant Tsukiji Outer Market with its fresh seafood stalls, street food vendors, and local shops. Try fresh sushi, grilled seafood, tamago (egg) skewers, and matcha desserts. Photography opportunity for colorful market scenes and food styling. Allow 90 minutes for browsing, eating, and shopping for unique souvenirs. The market is crowded but authentic and atmospheric.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsukiji Outer Market, Chuo Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Travel to Tokyo Station
timeline.detail: Take the Oedo Line or walk 15 minutes from Tsukiji to reach Tokyo Station. The iconic red-brick historic station building is a photography landmark. Allow 25 minutes total for travel and arrival. Regroup as a party of 6 at the main entrance or appointed meeting spot inside the station.
timeline.mapQuery: Tokyo Station, Chiyoda Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Tokyo Station Character Street Shopping
timeline.detail: Visit the Character Street located inside Tokyo Station basement (B1F). Browse anime, manga, and game character merchandise from Pokémon, Studio Ghibli, Hello Kitty, and more. Spend 60-90 minutes shopping for unique Japanese gifts and collectibles. This is an excellent photo location for company team photos with character backdrops and vibrant merchandise displays.
timeline.mapQuery: Character Street, Tokyo Station, Chiyoda Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Lunch at Tokyo Station Area
timeline.detail: Enjoy lunch at one of the many restaurants in Tokyo Station, including ramen shops, conveyor belt sushi, or bento box specialists. Popular options include Ichiran Ramen, Hanamaru Udon, or the station's sushi counters. Budget ¥1,500-2,500 per person. Groups of 6 can reserve or queue; estimated 30-45 minutes including seating and eating.
timeline.mapQuery: Tokyo Station, Ramen Street, Chiyoda Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Travel to Skytree or Tokyo Tower Viewing Deck
timeline.detail: Head to Tokyo Skytree (Solamachi Station, 15 minutes by train) or Tokyo Tower (Akabanebashi Station, 20 minutes). Skytree offers higher views (634m, 450m deck recommended). Purchase group tickets in advance online to save ¥1,000-1,500 per person. Ascend to viewing deck(s) for panoramic city photography and clear sightlines to Mount Fuji on clear days.
timeline.mapQuery: Tokyo Skytree, Sumida Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Mario Kart Experience & Activity
timeline.detail: Travel to Mario Kart: Ride @ Super Nintendo World (if at Universal Studios) or Mari Kart Street Kart rental in Shibuya/Roppongi (15-30 minutes from Skytree). Book group session in advance for 6 players. Ride in go-kart costumes through Tokyo streets for 90 minutes. Photographer will capture moments. Budget ¥6,000-8,000 per person including costume rental.
timeline.mapQuery: Mario Kart Street Kart, Shibuya Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Travel to Ginza & Pre-Dinner Walk
timeline.detail: Take metro from Mario Kart location to Ginza Station (15-20 minutes). Walk through Ginza's luxury shopping streets and historic architecture. Photograph iconic Ginza landmarks including the Wako clock tower and the main shopping avenue. Rest and prepare for dinner after an active afternoon. Window shopping and scouting dinner restaurant locations.
timeline.mapQuery: Ginza, Chuo Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Dinner in Ginza
timeline.detail: Enjoy dinner at a Ginza restaurant suitable for groups of 6. Options range from upscale kaiseki (¥8,000-15,000 per person), sushi counters (¥6,000-10,000), to sukiyaki/shabu-shabu restaurants (¥5,000-8,000 per person). Reserve in advance mentioning party size. Estimated duration 90 minutes. Ginza offers premium dining experiences with attentive service and refined Japanese cuisine.
timeline.mapQuery: Ginza, Chuo Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Return to Airbnb & Evening Relaxation
timeline.detail: Take the Marunouchi Line or Hibiya Line from Ginza back to Shinjuku Station, then walk 8 minutes to the Ōkubo Area Airbnb (approximately 25 minutes total). Arrive at accommodation by 20:45. Debrief the day with the team, review photos, and plan final day logistics. Use the washing machine if needed for laundry before departure on April 10th morning.
timeline.mapQuery: Ōkubo Area, Shinjuku City, Tokyo, Japan

localTips: Tsukiji Outer Market operates 06:00-14:00; best for fresh sushi breakfast (¥1,500-3,000 per meal).
Tokyo Station Character Street has 20+ licensed shops (Nintendo, Pokémon, Sanrio) — great for last-minute gifts.
Tokyo Skytree (¥2,100) or Tokyo Tower (¥900) for same-day visits; book timed entry online 24h ahead.
Mario Kart experience requires advance booking via activity platforms; 90-minute slots ¥6,000/person.
Ginza is best for dinner 17:30-19:30; arrive early to secure seating at popular standing sushi bars.

days.title: Departure Day
days.subtitle: Pack, shop, and journey back to Penang

timeline.title: Wake up and final packing
timeline.detail: Wake early to pack remaining items and check all belongings. Use the Airbnb washing machine if needed for any last-minute clothes. Ensure passports, travel documents, and duty-free receipts are organized in carry-on bags. Check weather forecast one final time and dress accordingly for airport journey. Confirm flight details and check-in procedures with group.
timeline.mapQuery: 1-chōme-9-17 Ōkubo, Shinjuku City, Tokyo Prefecture 169-0072, Japan

timeline.title: Checkout from Airbnb
timeline.detail: Complete final checkout from the Ōkubo Area Airbnb. Ensure all lights are off, windows are closed, and the property is locked securely. Return keys to host following checkout instructions. Do a final walkthrough to confirm no personal items are left behind. Take a group photo at the entrance as a trip memento.
timeline.mapQuery: 1-chōme-9-17 Ōkubo, Shinjuku City, Tokyo Prefecture 169-0072, Japan

timeline.title: Transport to Narita Airport
timeline.detail: Depart the Airbnb and travel to Narita Airport via Shinjuku Station using the Narita Express (N'EX) or airport shuttle bus, which takes approximately 60-90 minutes depending on traffic. Book tickets in advance as a group to secure seats. Confirm departure time with all 6 travelers and conduct a final headcount before leaving. Keep baggage organized and accessible during transport.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Station, Tokyo, Japan to Narita International Airport

timeline.title: Flight departure at 10:25am
timeline.detail: Arrive at the airport terminal and complete group check-in, distributing the 120kg luggage allowance efficiently across all 6 passengers. Present passports and flight documents to airline staff. Ensure all boarding passes are obtained and seats are confirmed. Pass through security screening and proceed to departure gate. Your flight departs at 10:25am for Penang.
timeline.mapQuery: Narita International Airport, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Boarding and departure
timeline.detail: Proceed to the departure gate for boarding. Present boarding passes and passports to gate staff. Ensure all 6 group members board together and are seated. Stow carry-on luggage in overhead compartments or under seats. Follow cabin crew safety instructions and settle in for the flight back to Penang. Reflect on the wonderful experiences, great food, stunning temples, and memorable moments shared during this Tokyo company trip adventure.
timeline.mapQuery: Narita International Airport, Tokyo to Penang, Malaysia

localTips: Depart Ōkubo Airbnb by 07:30am to catch N'EX train to Narita Airport to arrive 2 hours before departure.
Allow 2 hours for airport check-in and security; international flights depart 2 hours pre-flight.
Luggage allowance is 20kg per person (120kg total for 6 travellers); use coin lockers at stations if needed.
Keep all receipts from shopping — tax refund available for purchases over ¥5,000 at airport TAX REFUND counter.

hotels.name: Airbnb - Ōkubo Area
hotels.note: Fully equipped 6-person apartment with washing machine; 8 min walk to Shinjuku Station
hotels.address: 1-chōme-9-17 Ōkubo, Shinjuku City, Tokyo Prefecture 169-0072, Japan

budget.note: AirAsia or Malaysia Airlines, ~¥2,000 each via budget carriers
JR train ¥750 × 6 + bicycle rental ¥500 + attraction entries ¥1,500
Round-trip Busta Shinjuku coach ¥2,600 × 6 + Mt Fuji Ropeway ¥900 × 6

budget.item: Tokyo Skytree or Tokyo Tower (6 pax)
budget.note: Tokyo Skytree ¥2,100 × 6 or Tokyo Tower ¥900 × 6 (choose one)
90-minute race experience ¥6,000 × 6 (book via Klook or official site)
Budget ¥2,000/person/day (mix of convenience stores, ramen, sushi, restaurants)
Pokémon Center, Character Street, Akihabara anime merchandise, duty-free
Coin lockers, convenience items, contingency buffer

urgent.label: Book Busta Shinjuku Mt Fuji Coach
urgent.note: Mt Fuji day trip bus sells out during cherry blossom season; reserve 5-7 days ahead

urgent.label: Reserve Mario Kart Experience
urgent.note: Popular activity; book through Klook or official site with confirmed dates

urgent.label: Confirm Airbnb Ōkubo Check-in
urgent.note: Contact host 2 weeks before arrival to confirm check-in time and key pickup procedure

urgent.label: Book Tokyo Skytree Timed Entry
